headlines.title: U.S. revokes Iran oil sales authorization after tanker attacks
headlines.summary: Treasury had waived sanctions on Iranian oil through Aug. 21 after Washington and Tehran inked an interim deal to reopen Hormuz last month.

headlines.title: U.S. resumes 'powerful strikes' on Iran after Hormuz Strait ship attacks, CENTCOM says
headlines.summary: The U.S. revoked Iran oil sales authorization earlier in the day.

headlines.title: Maine Senate race replacements circle amid pressure on Platner to drop out
headlines.summary: Democrat Graham Platner has denied allegations of sexual assault leveled by Maine resident Jenny Racicot as calls for him to exit the pivotal race mount.

headlines.title: Netflix, Disney and YouTube interested in FIFA World Cup U.S. rights; package could reach $2 billion
headlines.summary: FIFA has alerted media companies that English- and Spanish-language U.S. rights are likely to be sold together for 2030 and 2034, likely driving up the price.

headlines.title: Trump renews Greenland threats at NATO summit, says U.S. could remove troops from Europe
headlines.summary: NATO fell into a crisis earlier this year as Trump demanded that the U.S. must take control of Greenland on national security grounds.

headlines.title: Meta enters AI image model race in bid to court advertisers and subscribers
headlines.summary: Meta has announced Muse Image, its first AI model for image creation, as it seeks to attract creators and advertisers to its offerings.

headlines.title: Kalshi traders see slim odds U.S. government will take a stake in OpenAI this year
headlines.summary: Traders on the prediction markets platform see a less than 30% likelihood the federal government will take a stake in AI companies Anthropic and OpenAI.

headlines.title: Buy these quality, low-stress stocks for the summer, says Jefferies
headlines.summary: This summer, Jefferies recommends focusing on some high-quality stocks that have been left behind by the AI momentum trade.

headlines.title: Here's how SpaceX's Nasdaq-100 inclusion might affect options pricing
headlines.summary: About half-a-million SpaceX options traded by midday Monday, a little below the average since inception.

headlines.title: Rivian, Tesla and the 'Holly Index': How to trade the new EV Main Street battle
headlines.summary: Mike Khouw gives a trade on Rivian and Tesla, informed by his wife's recent auto purchase.

headlines.title: Top Wall Street analysts prefer these dividend stocks for boosting portfolio returns
headlines.summary: With thousands of dividend-paying companies to choose from, identifying the right stocks can be challenging.

headlines.title: Tech bulls lose conviction as key trading metric blows out to the widest since 2008
headlines.summary: The pick-up in demand for puts aligns with slowing momentum in AI stocks that had been consistently rewarding speculators to the upside.

headlines.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
headlines.summary: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”

headlines.title: Micron’s stock falls as investors wonder if the memory market is near the top
headlines.summary: “Most investor feedback continues to point to a skittish AI tape,” one analyst said.

headlines.title: Adobe’s stock is temptingly cheap. Should investors bite?
headlines.summary: Adobe has become a polarizing stock in the face of questions about its AI future.

headlines.title: AI-related debt sells off sharply as Amazon looks to borrow another $25 billion
headlines.summary: Bonds financing the massive artificial-intelligence buildout were hit hard by selling on Tuesday, as Amazon.com set out to borrow another $25 billion in new debt.

headlines.title: Tesla’s stock could rise 20% thanks to the potential for a SpaceX merger, analyst says
headlines.summary: Some on Wall Street see an increasing likelihood that Elon Musk’s two biggest companies will combine.

headlines.title: Crude prices rise as U.S. launches strikes on Iran shortly after canceling its license to sell oil
headlines.summary: Oil futures climbed late Tuesday after the Treasury Department canceled a license granted on June 21 to allow for the sale of Iranian oil.

candidates.thesis: Advanced Micro Devices is a leader in high-performance computing and AI-driven semiconductor solutions, benefiting from secular growth in AI and compute infrastructure. Strong fundamentals and recent pullback make it attractive for long-term growth.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing is the world's largest and most advanced chip foundry, critical to global semiconductor supply chains and AI compute infrastructure. Trading near SMA50 with stable RSI, offering a favorable entry point.

ASML is the dominant supplier of lithography machines for semiconductor manufacturing, including EUV technology critical for advanced chips. Positioned to benefit from secular growth in AI and compute demand.

GEV is a clean energy company focused on small modular reactors (SMRs), a promising technology for the future of sustainable energy. Strong technical setup with RSI near neutral and trading above SMA50 and SMA200.

Rockwell Automation is a leader in industrial automation and digital transformation, benefiting from secular growth in smart manufacturing and IoT adoption. Stable technicals and favorable risk-reward setup.
